title: Extinction Learning Explained
script_summary: A brief exploration of extinction learning and its implications in psychology.

segments.voiceover: Here's what science actually says about extinction learning.
Extinction learning is the process where a learned response fades away after the reinforcement stops.
Imagine you’re afraid of dogs, but over time, you see they’re harmless, and your fear diminishes.
Studies show extinction learning can help in therapies for anxiety and PTSD by gradually reducing fear responses.
But here's the twist: preliminary research suggests extinction isn’t permanent. Feelings can resurface.
Want to unlock more secrets of the mind? Hit that follow button!
